steps.title: Isolate the needed support
steps.content: Morita and colleagues infer depth from oxygen-18 concentration in nautilid shell segments: F04 matches water around 90 meters deep, while M17 matches water around 385 meters deep. A supporting finding should make that oxygen-18 difference a reliable depth signal.

steps.title: Rule out sex as a factor
steps.content: Choice A shifts attention to sex and growth rate, not the depth inference. Choice B would make sex a possible alternative explanation for the oxygen-18 difference. Choice C says nautilids usually live deep but does not explain the two samples' different oxygen-18 levels. The remaining finding rules out sex as a confounding factor. The answer is D.
